immediately during setup instead of just writing a flag.

commit.message:
fix(service): use replaceView for seamless background/foreground video

Previously, backgrounding the app triggered surfaceDestroyed() which
called stopPreview(), killing Camera2 capture. On return, startPreview()
created a new RtmpCamera2/SrtCamera2 instance, orphaning the old one
with the active connection. Result: frozen video, audio continued.

Now StreamingService uses replaceView during active streams:
- detachPreviewSurface(): calls replaceViewWithBackground() to switch
  to headless mode (camera keeps capturing, encoder stays connected)
- attachPreviewSurface(): calls replaceView(openGlView) to hot-swap
  back to the new surface without interrupting the stream

stopPreview() is still used when no stream is active.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: StreamCaster-android
repository.description: StreamCaster is a native Android app for live streaming camera and/or microphone to RTMP/RTMPS endpoints.

commit.message:
feat(service): add replaceView methods to EncoderBridge for background streaming

Add replaceViewWithBackground(Context) and replaceView(OpenGlView) to
the EncoderBridge interface, wrapping RootEncoder's Camera2Base.replaceView()
API. This enables seamless background/foreground transitions:

- replaceViewWithBackground(): switches to headless GlStreamInterface,
  camera keeps capturing and encoder stays connected
- replaceView(OpenGlView): hot-swaps back to a visible surface mid-stream

Implemented in RtmpCamera2Bridge, SrtCamera2Bridge, and StubEncoderBridge.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add upsert-issue composite action (#55)

Create, update, reopen, or close a GitHub issue by title.
Searches for an existing issue with the exact title and manages
its lifecycle based on the 'open' input:

- open=true: create new issue or update+reopen existing
- open=false: close existing open issue or do nothing

Includes composite action, README, and test workflow.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add localization support (en-US + de-DE) (#4)

* feat: add localization support (en-US + de-DE)

- Add .resw resource files for English and German
- Add LocalizationService: reads/saves language override in LocalSettings,
  sets ApplicationLanguages.PrimaryLanguageOverride at startup
- Add LanguageOption model and SettingsViewModel
- Replace all hardcoded XAML strings with x:Uid
- Localize StatusText and GameEventType names in C# code
- Add language picker ComboBox + restart InfoBar to SettingsPage

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address Copilot PR review issues

- LanguageOption: remove hardcoded 'System Default'; display name now
  sourced from LanguageSystemDefault resource via LocalizationService
- LocalizationService: fix unsafe (string) cast in Initialize() with
  safe 'raw is string s' pattern; validate saved tag against known tags
  before applying PrimaryLanguageOverride; build SupportedLanguages with
  localized system-default display name after setting the override
- SettingsViewModel: use SupportedLanguages[0] fallback instead of
  removed LanguageOption.SystemDefault
- MainWindow: set Title via LocalizationService.GetString('AppTitle')
  in code-behind; add AppTitle resource to en-US and de-DE
- de-DE/Resources.resw: align ConfigTitle.Text to 'Konfiguration' to
  match NavConfig.Content

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: enhance localization support by refining language management and UI language persistence

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: flip playlist dropdown to open downward when near top of screen

Measures button's getBoundingClientRect().top on open; if < 200px from
top of viewport the menu uses top: calc(100% + 8px) instead of
bottom: calc(100% + 8px), preventing it from being hidden behind the
search bar on top search result rows.

Adds test covering the atpb-menu-down class is applied when button is
near top of screen.

Redesign remaining views with CSS modules and design system components

- Use Table component for list views (Finance, Sales, Ops, Audit, Knowledge)

- Use Skeleton for loading states in ApprovalView

- Redesign SettingsView with organized sections and integration grid
- Preserve all state, API calls, handlers, and logic exactly

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: rangspottr <265638228+rangspottr@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): resolve jq Infinity parse error — 5-point patch

Root cause: src/ had 50 files -> CALIBRATION_MILESTONE(=10) multiple
-> _check_calibration_hint() printed to stdout AFTER JSON output
-> jq parsed '{...json...}\n[*] Calibration milestone...' and failed

Fixes:
1. cli_commands.py: _check_calibration_hint() all print() -> file=sys.stderr
2. metrics/inflation.py: float('inf') -> 10.0 (max cap, RFC 8259 safe)
3. core.py: avg_inflation filter: status != 'error' -> math.isfinite()
4. cli.py: _sanitize_for_json() handles tuple; json.dumps(allow_nan=False)
5. tests/e2e_v321: milestone_fired/auto_calibrated check stdout+stderr

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Archetype setup wizard handoff pattern

Core federation-setup rewritten from scratch (v0.2.0):
- Stripped all archetype-specific config (deliverable, schema, steps, roles)
- Core config reduced to 4 fields: description, branchPrefix, mcpStack, telemetry
- Uses 'team' language, not 'domain'
- No upfront team listing — teams onboard one at a time
- After archetype install, hands off to archetype's setup skill

New archetype setup skills:
- deliverable-setup: filename, schema, playbook steps, import hook
- coding-setup: PR target branch, playbook steps, code conventions, test requirements

Both write to .squad/archetype-config.json (team worktree), not core config.
Handoff: core setup → install archetype → archetype setup activates.

Co-authored-by: Vladi Lyga <lyvladi@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
style(ContextPrimitivesView): use default text color in summary block

Remove selectedBlock.fg color overrides from the detail panel so
text always renders in the default foreground color.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove @media dark override that bled dark vars into light mode

The @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) block in :root was overriding CSS
variables to dark values even when the user had manually selected light mode
via the toggle (localStorage = '0'). Because the html.dark class was absent,
element-level dark overrides did not apply — but the dark :root vars did.
Result: dark backgrounds/text in light mode for OS-dark-mode users.

Fix: remove the CSS media query block entirely. Theme is now controlled
exclusively by the JS class toggle (html.dark) which already reads OS
preference via matchMedia in initDarkMode(). No behaviour change for
first-time visitors; manual overrides are now respected correctly.

Bonus fix: toggleDarkMode() was replacing btn.textContent with emoji text,
wiping the Lucide <i> icon node. Extracted _darkBtnSync() helper that
updates only the <span> text and swaps the data-lucide attribute.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: xzyaoi <10499297+xzyaoi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor Docker and CI/CD configurations, remove deprecated scripts, and enhance logging

- Updated GitHub Actions workflow to use new Dockerfile for amd64 architecture and set build metadata.
- Added new Dockerfile for building signed OpenTela binaries.
- Removed obsolete deployment scripts and configuration files for Clariden and Euler.
- Updated Dockerfile to streamline the build process and ensure proper entry points.
- Enhanced logging in the protocol to provide more detailed debug information.
- Adjusted public address handling to support DNS hostnames in the network configuration.

* baremetal docker serving docs

* feat(fleet_manager): add proxychains support + migrate cluster configs

Add ProxyChains dataclass to cluster.py with enabled/ssh_key/proxy_target/
socks_port/skip_partitions fields. When enabled and the preset partition is
not in skip_partitions, apptainer_single/multi templates open an SSH SOCKS
tunnel from inside the SLURM allocation and export HTTPS_PROXY/HTTP_PROXY/
ALL_PROXY so downstream commands (apptainer pull, HF hub, user code) have
internet access via the tunnel.

Populate contrib/fleet_manager/clusters/ with jsc, jsc-dev, euler, clariden
migrated to the preset-based schema. JSC enables proxychains by default with
develbooster and dc-gpu-devel listed in skip_partitions.

Update README to document presets, user-command env vars ($SERVICE_PORT,
$HF_HOME), multi-node conventions, and proxychains. Delete stale docs/
files that described the old model-centric layout.

52/52 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* update deploy playbook

commit.message:
fix(ClosingQuoteView): use canonical URL for social share links

LinkedIn and Facebook only support passing a URL to their share dialogs
(text pre-fill is not supported). Both platforms pull title, description,
and image from the page's OG tags.

Replace window.location.href with the hardcoded canonical URL so shares
always point to the production page and OG tags are reliably scraped.

feat: add quality comparison script and timing output to pipeline

- scripts/run_quality_comparison.py: multi-backend E2E comparison across
  Python + 5 Rust backends (LibTorch f32/bf16, CUDA f32/bf16, WGPU f32);
  generates performance_report.md and quality_comparison.zip
  to tracing log for reliable programmatic parsing; adds t_sample/t_decode
  wall-clock timing using std::time::Instant
- justfile: add quality-compare, quality-compare-fast, quality-compare-backend
  recipes

Results summary:
- Python PyTorch:      RF ~3200ms
- Rust LibTorch f32:   RF ~4000ms (1.25x slower, duration delta <8%)
- Rust LibTorch bf16:  RF ~2230ms (1.43x faster than Python)
- CUDA bf16: broken (burn-ir tensor handle teardown panic, pre-release bug)
- CUDA f32/WGPU: work but slow due to CubeCL per-process JIT autotuning

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix gap below reply button when reply box is collapsed

Collapsed .reply-row had flex: 0 0 100% which claimed its own flex
line; the container's row-gap then added 6px of blank space after the
buttons even when the reply box was hidden.

flex: 0 0 100% is restored when the .open class is toggled on.

commit.message:
Fix skill activation: SessionStart hook + @federation agent

Problem: Squad's coordinator agent (squad.agent.md) intercepts all user
messages before plugin skills activate. 'Set up a team organization'
triggers Squad's Init Mode, not our federation-setup skill.

Solution (combined approach):
- Option A: SessionStart hook injects federation awareness into the
  coordinator's context. Lists specific topics to delegate to federation
  skills instead of handling as normal Squad init. Works immediately.
- Option B variant: @federation agent for explicit invocation. User can
  say '@federation set up a team organization' to bypass the coordinator
  entirely. Always available, no competition.

Co-authored-by: Vladi Lyga <lyvladi@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  with other action buttons. Clicking it slides open the text
  input + Send button below (max-height transition, auto-focuses
  the input). Clicking again collapses it.
- Removed the always-visible full-width reply row that was forcing
  a line-break in the action area even before any reply was needed.
- Tightened action button padding (5px 12px → 4px 10px) and font
  size (13px → 12px) with white-space:nowrap so more buttons fit
  on a single line before wrapping.
- Added align-items:center and reduced gap (8px → 6px) to the
  actions flex container.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: robustify RptRequirementHandler JSON parsing (#225)

- Dispose JsonDocument with 'using' to release pooled memory promptly
- Use TryGetProperty for 'permissions', 'rsname', and 'scopes' so
  incomplete or non-standard RPT payloads skip gracefully instead
  of throwing KeyNotFoundException at runtime
- Add RptRequirementHandlerTests covering all new code paths

commit.message:
Fix: add tap overlay so double-tap works over iframes

Iframes capture all touch events, preventing the hub document
from receiving double-tap. Added transparent overlay with z-index
above iframes to reliably capture touch/click events.
